intro.prefix: These rules are based on the
intro.bold: FIFA 2026 World Cup Official Competition Regulations (approved 2024)
intro.suffix: . The 2026 tournament features 48 nations divided into 12 groups of 4. The top two teams from each group plus the eight best third-placed teams advance to the Round of 32 (knockout stage).

1.label: Points
1.sub: The team with the most points takes precedence.

2.label: Goal difference (overall)
2.sub: Goals scored minus goals conceded across all group matches.

3.label: Goals scored (overall)
3.sub: Total goals scored across all group matches.

4-6.label: Head-to-head
4-6.sub: Apply only the records between tied teams → ④ head-to-head points → ⑤ head-to-head goal difference → ⑥ head-to-head goals scored. If three or more teams are tied and head-to-head separates only some, repeat ④–⑥ for the remaining tied teams.

7.label: Fair play points
7.sub: The team with fewer disciplinary points (cards) takes precedence (see card penalty table below).

8.label: FIFA ranking
8.sub: Higher official FIFA ranking points take precedence.

9.label: Drawing of lots
9.sub: If all criteria above are equal, FIFA decides by public draw.

fairPlay.title: Fair play penalty table
fairPlay.intro: Lower is better. The team with fewer total penalty points takes precedence.
fairPlay.footer: Administrative sanctions such as suspensions are imposed on the player and do not affect the team's fair play score.

thirdPlace.title: Third-place qualification criteria

intro.prefix: Among the third-placed teams from the 12 groups, the

thirdPlace.subtitle: Third-place ranking criteria (same as second-place comparison)
thirdPlace.note: Head-to-head is not applied between third-placed teams since they play in different groups.

knockout.title: Round of 32 structure

intro.prefix: 12 group winners + 12 runners-up + top 8 third-placed teams =

knockout.note: The Round of 32 matchups (which group winner faces which third-placed team) are determined by the FIFA official bracket based on which groups the qualifying third-placed teams come from.

source: Source: FIFA World Cup 2026 – Competition Regulations (approved 2024) · FIFA.com
